title: BOM round-trip via Win-1252 corrupts first character to '?'
description: Convert-LegacyEncoding (P023 repair path) decoded UTF-8 bytes including the leading BOM (EF BB BF -> U+FEFF) and re-encoded the resulting string as Windows-1252. Win-1252 has no codepoint for U+FEFF, so the encoder substitutes 0x3F ('?'). When the bytes are re-decoded as UTF-8 and re-saved with a fresh BOM, the file ends up with '?' as the first body character, silently corrupting `# VersionTag` or `<#` comment headers and producing parser errors several lines later. Observed: scripts/Build-AgenticManifest.ps1 became unparseable after a 'successful' P023 repair.
fix_strategy: Strip the UTF-8 BOM (first 3 bytes EF BB BF) BEFORE round-tripping through Windows-1252.
Re-prepend BOM (or let UTF8Encoding($true) writer add it) only on the final write.
Add a post-fix parse check: any P023 repair must verify the file still parses with [Parser]::ParseFile and revert from backup on failure.

first_seen.outcome: Parser errors on line 38 (Unexpected attribute 'CmdletBinding'); root cause was stray '?' after BOM
